pr.title: [WIP] Update uuid package to version 11.1.1 to fix vulnerability
pr.body:
Thanks for asking me to work on this. I will get started on it and keep this PR's description up to date as I form a plan and make progress.

> You are a security analyst for the Renumics organization.
> Analyze the repository "lexio".
> 
> ## Open Dependabot alerts
> 
> [
>   {
>     "number": 186,
>     "severity": "medium",
>     "package": "uuid",
>     "title": "uuid: Missing buffer bounds check in v3/v5/v6 when buf is provided",
>     "fixed_in": "11.1.1"
>   }
> ]
> 
> ## Open code-scanning alerts
> 
> []
> 
> ## Workflow
> 
> 1. Fetch all open Dependabot and code-scanning alerts for this repository.
>    Focus on alerts from the last 24 hours.
> 2. For each alert (prioritize critical/high):
>    - Search the repo code for the vulnerable package or affected code path
>    - Assess blast radius: external exposure, user data, reachability
>    - Determine if the vulnerable code path is actually used in production
> 3. For alerts with relevant (reachable) blast radius:
>    - If a fix version is available, create a pull request updating the dependency
>    - If no fix exists but risk is real, add a comment on the alert explaining the exposure
> 4. If any alerts have relevant blast radius, create a single issue titled
>    "Daily Security Report" with this format:
> 
>    For each alert with relevant blast radius:
>    - 🔴/🟠/🟡 **[blast radius] Package/Rule**
>      - Affected: files or code paths
>      - Blast radius: one line
>      - Action: upgrade to X.Y.Z / no fix / low risk
> 
> 5. If NO alerts have relevant blast radius (all are unreachable, dev-only,
>    or low risk), do NOT create an issue. Skip silently.
> 6. Do not report low blast radius alerts.
pr.author: Copilot
